sections.paragraph:
δισσός, ή, όν (s. next entry; Trag., Pla., X. et al.; PTebt 27, 53 et al. pap; LXX; ParJer 7:30; AssMos Fgm. f [διττ-] p. 65 Denis; Philo, Aet. M. 20; SibOr 1, 327; Ath. 19, 2) pert. to being twice in quantity, whether similar or different in kind, double δ. ἕξουσιν τὴν κρίσιν they will incur double punishment 2 Cl 10:5; δ. φόβοι two kinds of fear Hm 7:4.—Frisk s.v. δί.

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

AssMos AssMos = Assumption of Moses—List 2

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Denis Denis = AD., Fragmenta pseudepigraphorum quae supersunt Graeca—Lists 2 beg., 5, 6

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Hm Hm = Mandates

Frisk Frisk = HF., Griechisches etymologisches Wörterbuch—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 252
